blog_title: 10 Things to Buy in the Shopaholic’s Paradise
seo_title: 10 Things to Buy in the Shopaholic’s Paradise
description: Discover the ultimate travel packing list for every adventure. Pack smart, light, and stress-free with essential tips .
body_content:
Published by travelxploria on Mon Jun 23 2025

Dubai, the shimmering jewel of the UAE, is often dubbed the “shopaholic’s paradise.” With its extravagant malls, traditional souks, and myriad of unique shops, it beckons those who want to indulge in retail therapy. For travelers from Kolkata, Dubai offers a wealth of shopping experiences that blend luxury with tradition. If you are planning a trip, here are 10 must-buy items that will ensure you take a piece of Dubai back home with you.

Persian Rugs and Carpets

Renowned for their intricate designs and craftsmanship, these rugs add a touch of elegance to any home. Visit the carpet souks and choose from a wide array of styles, colors, and sizes.

Arabian Coffee and Coffee Pots

Arabian coffee, known as “Gahwa,” is a staple in Emirati culture. Buying authentic Arabian coffee along with traditional coffee pots (Dallah) is a perfect way to experience the region’s hospitality. These items make for excellent gifts and meaningful souvenirs.

Arabic Attars

Arabic attars, or perfumes, are natural and alcohol-free fragrances made from flowers, herbs, and spices. The rich scents of these attars are unique to the region and can be found in beautifully ornate bottles. These serve as perfect gifts or personal treats.

Camel Milk Chocolate

This unique Dubai delicacy is creamier and has a slightly different flavor profile compared to regular chocolate. Available in various forms, including bars and truffles, it is a delightful souvenir for friends and family.

Arabic Calligraphy Art

These stunning pieces showcase the beauty of Arabic culture and often feature verses from the Quran or traditional Arabic sayings, beautifully crafted and framed. These make for meaningful decorations in any home.

Arabian Dates and Other Dried Fruits

Dates are a significant part of Arabian culture, and Dubai offers a variety of delicious dates. Consider buying premium dates, often filled with nuts or coated in chocolate, alongside other dried fruits to take back home a taste of the region.

Arabic Lanterns

Colorful Arabic lanterns represent traditional Middle Eastern decor. Available in various sizes and designs, these can be a stunning addition to your home.

Traditional Arab Shoes

Traditional Arab shoes, known as “Bakhoor,” are handcrafted and often feature intricate designs. These comfortable, stylish footwear pieces are perfect for casual outings.

Precious and Semi-Precious Stones

Dubai is famous for its jewelry, particularly precious and semi-precious stones. Whether you are looking for a statement piece or a unique gift, the jewelry souks offer an extensive selection of exquisite items that capture the essence of Dubai.


Handmade Crafts

Pottery, textiles, intricate handmade jewelry and other handmade craft items reflect the rich culture and artistry of the UAE, making them perfect souvenirs for your travels.

FAQ

How much Dubai trip will cost?

It varies, according to personal preferences regarding flights, accommodation, activities etc.

How much does a 7-day trip to Dubai cost India?

A 7-day trip to Dubai from India generally costs around ₹65,000 – ₹1, 50,000. However, it actually depends on the choice of flights, hotels, meals, activities etc.

How many days are good for Dubai trip?

5 – 7 days is ideal for a Dubai trip, allowing ample time to explore attractions, shopping, and cultural experiences.

With a diverse range of products that reflect its rich heritage and modern luxury, Dubai is a shopper’s dream destination.
